text: A systematic review in Springer Archives of Computational Methods in Engineering (September 2025) categorized AI-driven archaeology into computer vision, deep learning, geospatial analysis, and NLP, with reported accuracies exceeding 94% on ceramic typology classification tasks.

AI-powered satellite and LiDAR imagery analysis has revolutionized archaeological site prospection, with one 2024 project identifying over 300 new Nazca geoglyphs using a ResNet-based classifier on high-resolution satellite imagery. Deep learning models trained on known site locations can predict the probability of archaeological presence across large geographic areas.
